text.title: django-quality-logging Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end worked example: greenfield Django 5.2 + Python 3.12 project gets structlog + Sentry from zero to passing `check --deploy`.

example.code:
# Step 1: audit (greenfield — nothing installed) $ grep -r "structlog\|sentry_sdk" config/ # (no matches)
# Step 2: install $ uv add 'structlog>=24' 'django-structlog>=10' 'sentry-sdk>=2'
# Step 3: wire structlog (copy templates/logging.py into config/settings/base.py) # adds: MIDDLEWARE += ["django_structlog.middlewares.RequestMiddleware"] # adds: LOGGING dict with json/console formatters # adds: structlog.configure(...) call
# Step 4: wire sentry (copy templates/sentry_init.py into config/settings/production.py) $ export SENTRY_DSN="https://<key>@<org>.ingest.sentry.io/<project>"
# Step 5: gate $ python manage.py check --deploy --fail-level WARNING --settings=config.settings.production System check identified no issues (0 silenced).
# Step 6: emit report → run validator $ python scripts/validate-django-quality-logging.py .aidocs/F-099/logging-audit.md PASS
example.note: Resulting log line example: `{"event":"order_created","request_id":"3f9b1a","user_id":42,"order_id":7,"timestamp":"2026-05-22T15:00:00Z","level":"info","logger":"orders.services"}` — request_id and user_id auto-bound by RequestMiddleware; order_id and event explicitly bound in the service function.
